blocks.text: Engagement Letter: Pricing Study for Johnson PLC
Ms. Melissa Sexton, VP Finance, Johnson PLC
Dear Ms. Sexton:

This letter sets out the terms of the pricing study Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC will conduct for {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}. Sign and return the enclosed copy, and we will hold the start date of {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}} for you.

The question you put to us is the right one: whether your prices reflect what your products are worth, and where your discounting gives that value back. Our study will answer it. We will build the price and margin picture from your own invoice records, walk the discounting practice account by account, and set your list and realized prices against the competitors your team follows. What you will receive is not a binder of charts. You will receive a pricing structure your leadership can defend, a discount policy your sales organization can follow, and the analysis behind both. The work will run roughly seven months, and you will hear from us throughout. We report as we learn, not only at the end.

Jenna Wiley, Principal Consultant, will run the study day to day and will be your first call on any question. Lisa Hernandez, Analyst, will build and test the numbers. I review every page before it reaches you, and I sign what we send. You will always know whose name stands behind the work.

We will need access to invoice-level data and time with the people who set and approve prices. Jenna will send the opening data request once we begin, and we will keep the burden on your staff as light as the work allows. Anything you hand us stays inside this engagement.

Our fee is fixed at {{fact:f:E-2020-001.fee}} and covers all professional time. Out-of-pocket expenses, if any, are billed at cost. Work begins on {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}}, once your signed copy is in hand.
Johnson PLC will have our full attention. If anything in this letter reads differently than you understood it, call me before you sign and we will set it right.
Sincerely,
Kickoff Memo: Pricing Study for Johnson PLC
To: Lisa Hernandez. From: Jenna Wiley. Re: Kickoff for the {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} pricing study.

Johnson PLC has signed, and we start {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}}. The brief in one sentence: tell them what their pricing should be, where their discounting leaks, and what to change first, in a form their leadership can act on. Our client contact is Melissa Sexton, VP Finance. She reads carefully and skims nothing that involves a number, so every exhibit ties before it leaves the building. Findings first in everything we write; she can pull the support from the appendix if she wants it.
Workstreams

items: Data build. Lisa assembles the invoice-level price and margin dataset from the client's records and reconciles it before any analysis rests on it.
Discount practice. Lisa maps who approves what off list and where realized price departs from it; I review the account-level findings before they go anywhere.
Competitive position. I build the comparison against the competitors the client's team follows, with their input on the comparison set.
Recommendations. I draft the pricing structure and discount policy options; the Managing Partner reviews and signs anything that goes to the client.

blocks.text: Next steps

items: Lisa: draft the opening data request for my review so it goes out the day we start.
Me: schedule the first working session with Melissa Sexton's office.
Both: reread the engagement letter before the first client call. The scope is the scope.

blocks.text: Meeting Minutes: Pricing Study for Johnson PLC
Working session, held by videoconference. Minutes prepared by Lisa Hernandez, Analyst, and circulated the same afternoon. Corrections are welcome and should be sent to me directly.
Attendees

items: Jenna Wiley, Principal Consultant, Pinebrook Advisory Group
Lisa Hernandez, Analyst, Pinebrook Advisory Group (minutes)
Melissa Sexton, VP Finance, Johnson PLC

blocks.text: Discussion

Jenna Wiley reviewed progress on the pricing study for {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}. The invoice-level dataset is assembled and reconciled, and the margin analysis is underway. Early indications, which should probably be treated as preliminary until the account-level review is complete, are that discounting is concentrated in a relatively small set of accounts.

Melissa Sexton asked how the competitive comparison would treat products without a direct competitor equivalent. Jenna proposed grouping them by nearest substitute, and Melissa Sexton agreed, with the caveat that her team review the groupings before the analysis is finalized. It was also agreed, or at least generally understood, that pricing recommendations will go to Melissa Sexton for a factual read before any wider distribution at the client.
Action items

rows: Complete the account-level discount review and circulate preliminary observations internally
Send the proposed product groupings to the client team for review
Confirm the client-side reviewers for the competitive groupings
